Antarctica was dedicated to "all the polar bears in denial" and contained Daily Blues quotes and Ice V and Block of Ice (Osees) teases. Ice V contained Head On/Pill and Blindness (The Fall) teases. Gamma Knife contained Converge teases.  People-Vultures contained Iron Lung teases & quotes and Phantom Island quotes. Stu said "Free Palestine" before Boogieman Sam, which contained an Eternal Return vocal jam, Am I In Heaven? teases and a Riff Raff (AC/DC) tease. Slow Jam 1 contained Am I In Heaven? teases. Am I In Heaven? was unfinished and contained a Sad Pilot jam and Riff Raff (AC/DC) teases. The intro to Self-Immolate contained Am I In Heaven? teases and a drum solo where Stu, Joey, and an audience member shaved most of Stu's head.

jamchart_notes: Featured early and often Hypertension teases, extensive jamming, and weaving in between both songs.
shownotes: Iron Lung contained a Hypertension intro and teases. Hypertension contained Am I In Heaven?, Hot Water, Evil Death Roll, Boogieman Sam, and The River teases and Boogieman Sam quotes. Fans Silvia and David provided the vocal intro on Nuclear Fusion. Evil Death Roll was introduced as a song about crocodiles and contained Interior People and Hypertension teases. Digital Black was introduced as a song about the near future. Le Risque contained teases of La Grange (ZZ Top) and The Bitter Boogie. Welcome To An Altered Future was on the printed setlist but not played.

The Dripping Tap was unfinished. Rattlesnake contained Nuclear Fusion and Sleep Drifter teases. After Rattlesnake, Ambrose quoted an old Australian ad. Flamethrower featured a drum solo by Cavs. Extinction and Set featured the debut of the upgraded synth table (Nathan). Set contained Extinction quotes and Temporary Secretary (Paul McCartney) and The River teases. The River contained Thunderstruck (AC/DC) and Sad Pilot teases.

shownotes: The band walkout music was H.O.O.D by Kneecap. Theia was performed in an alternate rock version for the first time and contained a The Land Before Timeland tease and Wah Wah teases. Wah Wah contained a The River intro, quote, and teases as well as a Crumbling Castle tease and Motor Spirit teases and quotes. Open Water featured a drum solo by Cavs. Oddlife, which was played for the first time since 2023-03-13 (114 show gap), was played for Cookie and introduced as his favorite Gizzard song. I'm Not In Your Mind contained a Theia (Extended) jam with lyrics and Cellophane teases and featured Ambrose on sax. After Gila Monster, Joey said Free Palestine.
venuename: Plaza Mayor de El Poble Espanyol

shownotes: Supreme Ascendancy was played for the first time since 2023-08-16 (84 shows). The Lord of Lightning contained Am I In Heaven? and I'm In Your Mind teases. The Balrog contained Forty Six & 2 (Tool) and Am I In Heaven? teases. You Can Be Your Silhouette was introduced as a song about "being who you want to be and loving yourself." Cut Throat Boogie featured a harmonica/guitar duel between Ambrose and Joey and contained a La Grange (ZZ Top) jam and The Bitter Boogie teases. Daily Blues contained a Le Risque jam and Boogieman Sam teases. The Grim Reaper contained H.O.O.D (Kneecap) and Intergalactic (Beastie Boys) quotes and Ambrose saying Free Palestine. Following The Grim Reaper, Joey said it was "our nu-metal moment", which was followed by Cavs teasing and Joey quoting My Generation (Limp Bizkit). Following Superbug, Joey shouted out opener Etran de L'Aïr and mentioned an afterparty happening later and Ambrose mentioned playing songs by Australian musicians Slim Dusty, John Farnham and Jimmy Barnes. Following Flamethrower, Joey said Free Palestine.
venuename: Plaza Mayor de El Poble Espanyol

Following Superbug, Joey shouted out opener Etran de L'Aïr and mentioned an afterparty happening later and Ambrose mentioned playing songs by Australian musicians Slim Dusty, John Farnham and Jimmy Barnes. Following Flamethrower, Joey said Free Palestine.
venuename: Plaza Mayor de El Poble Espanyol

artist: King Gizzard & The Lizard Wizard DJ's
shownotes: DJ set by Ambrose, Cavs, Cook and Joey. BUSSIT (Joey Valence & Brae) was played very briefly and was possibly accidental. Ambrose jumped on a chair and was held up by Cavs and Joey during Dog Dribble (Getdown Services). Cavs rode on Joey's shoulders during U Should Not Be Doing That (Amyl and the Sniffers). Cavs poured beer into the mouths of the front row during Dragula (Rob Zombie). A song was briefly played though it seemed to be an accident as Joey explained that they were having technical difficulties and asked for requests. Joey imitated Brian Johnson during Thunderstruck (AC/DC). Afterwards he asked the audience for requests which included Never Gonna Give You Up by Rick Astley and anything by George Michael, Nine Inch Nails, James Brown, Bill Withers and Green Day. During Rush (Troye Sivan) Joey said that Troye Sivan was supposed to show up to King Gizzard's show on 2025-05-23. Joey said "Free Palestine" during Basha (Hassan Abou Alam). Ambrose said he "fucked up" You Know How We Do It (Ice Cube) and chose You Can Do It (Ice Cube) instead. Before 1 Thing (Amerie) Ambrose said that it was his favorite song of all time and that it has the best drum sound ever. Ambrose introduced Thong Song (Sisqo) by explaining the Australian meaning of thong (flip-flop) and said that it always confused him. Ambrose said that Toxic (Britney Spears) was the best song ever, said "Free Britney" and then said he wished he was as badass as her. Before Party All the Time (Eddie Murphy) Ambrose introduced Cook to the stage and called him his best friend, which Cook agreed with. Ambrose also said that its Cook's favorite song.

Cavs poured beer into the mouths of the front row during Dragula (Rob Zombie). A song was briefly played though it seemed to be an accident as Joey explained that they were having technical difficulties and asked for requests. Joey imitated Brian Johnson during Thunderstruck (AC/DC). Afterwards he asked the audience for requests which included Never Gonna Give You Up by Rick Astley and anything by George Michael, Nine Inch Nails, James Brown, Bill Withers and Green Day. During Rush (Troye Sivan) Joey said that Troye Sivan was supposed to show up to King Gizzard's show on 2025-05-23. Joey said "Free Palestine" during Basha (Hassan Abou Alam). Ambrose said he "fucked up" You Know How We Do It (Ice Cube) and chose You Can Do It (Ice Cube) instead. Before 1 Thing (Amerie) Ambrose said that it was his favorite song of all time and that it has the best drum sound ever. Ambrose introduced Thong Song (Sisqo) by explaining the Australian meaning of thong (flip-flop) and said that it always confused him. Ambrose said that Toxic (Britney Spears) was the best song ever, said "Free Britney" and then said he wished he was as badass as her. Before Party All the Time (Eddie Murphy) Ambrose introduced Cook to the stage and called him his best friend, which Cook agreed with. Ambrose also said that its Cook's favorite song.

Lucas' kid Monty was brought on stage before the show. Gaia featured a short drum solo by Cavs. Following the conclusion of Gaia Cookie quoted In the Jailhouse Now (Jimmie Rodgers) with lyrics changed to "in the big house now."  1 Thing (Amerie) was quoted before This Thing. Ambrose was given a police cap during Field of Vision which he wore for the rest of the song and engaged in police related antics with band photographer Maclay Heriot. Sad Pilot contained an Antarctica tease, teases of Head On/Pill and Hot Water, and a tease of The River. Gamma Knife contained Motor Spirit teases.  Before Work This Time Ambrose quoted Work It (Missy Elliott).  Shanghai contained Grow Wings and Fly teases and Slow Jam 1 quotes. Slow Jam 1 was performed in an alternate synth version for the first time and featured Ambrose on saxophone.  Swan Song was unfinished, contained Slow Jam 1 quotes and Shanghai teases and was followed by a My Generation (Limp Bizkit) tease by Cavs.  Head On/Pill featured Ambrose playing "slide harmonica" on Stu's guitar and Lewis Stiles of King Stingray drumming with Cavs, and contained Sea of Trees quotes.
soundcheck: Gamma Knife, Iron Lung (partial), Slow Jam 1 (synth version), Shanghai, Work This Time (no jam), Sleepwalker (partial), Magenta Mountain (partial)

shownotes: This show featured the debuts of Jailbreak (AC/DC) and the synth outro of Flamethrower. Ambrose sung along to the walkout music, which was Party All the Time (Eddie Murphy). Antarctica contained Hot Water and Forty Six and 2 (Tool) teases. Before Hypertension, the band talked about Stu's new guitar, mentioning Jimi Hendrix and Dimebag Darrell, and joking that they wanted to play Little Wing on the table, but Cavs said no because he wanted to play Foxy Lady and Cookie wanted to play She's So Fine. Boogieman Sam was teased before Ice V, which contained Blindness (The Fall) teases. Ambrose quoted “Good day, good evening, and good night!” from The Truman Show before Rats In The Sky. Jailbreak was impromptu/not on the written setlist, unfinished, and contained a Rats In the Sky outro. The Fourth Colour was on the printed setlist after Crumbling Castle but was not played.
soundcheck: Motor Spirit, Crumbling Castle > The Fourth Colour

Antarctica contained Hot Water and Forty Six and 2 (Tool) teases. Before Hypertension, the band talked about Stu's new guitar, mentioning Jimi Hendrix and Dimebag Darrell, and joking that they wanted to play Little Wing on the table, but Cavs said no because he wanted to play Foxy Lady and Cookie wanted to play She's So Fine. Boogieman Sam was teased before Ice V, which contained Blindness (The Fall) teases. Ambrose quoted “Good day, good evening, and good night!” from The Truman Show before Rats In The Sky. Jailbreak was impromptu/not on the written setlist, unfinished, and contained a Rats In the Sky outro. The Fourth Colour was on the printed setlist after Crumbling Castle but was not played.

shownotes: Am I In Heaven? contained Antarctica and Hypertension teases. Self-Immolate featured a drum solo. Dragon featured a fan named Ignas on Stu’s guitar instead of Stu. After Dragon, Joey wished Stu a happy Pink Soup Day (a local festival in Vilnius about beet soup). Billabong Valley contained a See Me quote. Stu said "Fuck Putin" during Pleura. Le Risque was introduced as being called "Pink Soup", to which the band started chanting "Pink Soup", and Joey vocally teased Kernkraft 400 (Zombie Nation). The song itself featured a fan dressed as Evel Knievel on stage during Ambrose's verse, who Stu thanked after the song. Down The Sink was played for the first time since 2023-08-30 (76 show gap), in a new alternate synthpop arrangement, and contained Gilgamesh quotes. As Gilgamesh ended, Joey compared the band's synth playing at the end of the jam to Rammstein. Joey and Cavs teased Blind (Korn) before Trapdoor. The Dripping Tap featured Stu telling a story about him and Joey getting pink soup and how it was the best thing they had ever eaten, with Joey playing his guitar with his teeth because he "spilled pink soup on it", and contained a Tool jam featuring elements and variations of Forty Six & 2 and Schism with Motor Spirit quotes. After The Dripping Tap, Joey said "Free Palestine," "Glory to Ukraine" (from a sign in the audience) and "humanity."

footnote: With fan Ignas on Stu’s guitar
jamchart_notes: Dragon featured a fan named Ignas on Stu’s Holy Explorer guitar. The band invited him on stage after seeing his sign with a request to play Dragon. He notably crushed the particularly difficult guitar part.

Altered Beast I was played for the first time since 2023-06-16 (96 shows), Alter Me I was played for the first time since 2024-05-18 (64 shows), and Altered Beast II was played for the first time since 2024-03-14 (72 shows). Raw Feel contained Am I In Heaven? and Head On/Pill teases. Boogieman Sam featured a guitar/harmonica duel between Joey & Amby and contained teases of Sad Pilot & The Bitter Boogie, a Mirage City jam, Altered Beast II teases and Phantom Island quotes. Chang'e was unfinished. After Hell, Joey introduced the rest of the band, followed by Stu introducing Joey. The audience then chanted "free free Palestine!"; the band encouraged them to "say that shit louder!".
soundcheck: Altered Beast Suite, Venusian 1, Predator X, Mirage City

Boogieman Sam featured a guitar/harmonica duel between Joey & Amby and contained teases of Sad Pilot & The Bitter Boogie, a Mirage City jam, Altered Beast II teases and Phantom Island quotes. Chang'e was unfinished. After Hell, Joey introduced the rest of the band, followed by Stu introducing Joey. The audience then chanted "free free Palestine!"; the band encouraged them to "say that shit louder!".
soundcheck: Altered Beast Suite, Venusian 1, Predator X, Mirage City

shownotes: I'm Not In Your Mind contained a The Dripping Tap jam with lyrics and Alter Me I teases and quotes. Cellophane contained Hog Calling Contest and Am I In Heaven? teases and featured Ambrose on sax. I'm In Your Mind Fuzz contained a tease of The Balrog.  Iron Lung contained Phantom Island quotes. Evil Death Roll contained Hypertension teases, Iron Lung teases and quotes, and Smoke & Mirrors quotes. Cavs played the drumbeat from Bitter Boogie in Muddy Water and vice versa, and each contained teases from the other, with Bitter Boogie ending in a brief Muddy Water reprise. One of the band's sound engineers, Joe Santarpia, briefly joined the band onstage to perform a pig noise during Hog Calling Contest. Kelper-22b was played for the first time since 2023-09-02 (76 show gap), and in a new electronic arrangement.
soundcheck: Ambergris, Hog Calling Contest, The Book, Ataraxia, Superposition, Kepler-22b

shownotes: This show featured the debut of Superposition. The Fourth Colour contained a Dripping Tap jam with lyrics. Band manager Michelle's son Laurence joined the band on stage for Straws in the Wind, which contained Phantom Island quotes and Ambrose chanting "free free Palestine". The audience continued the Palestine chants after the song, with the band repeating the slogan once along with them and then again during Minimum Brain Size. Before Nuclear Fusion, Joey thanked King Stingray and DJ Crenshaw. A fan named Tom provided the vocal intro on Nuclear Fusion wearing a Bullant shirt. The rock version of Theia was performed, which contained a Land Before Timeland jam and Iron Lung teases & quotes. Before Ice V, which was dedicated to "the incredible country called Greece", Joey told the story of his appearance in the Ice V music video, which was filmed on the island of Idra. The song itself contained Ain't It Funky Now (James Brown) teases. Digital Black was preceded by a tease of A New World. Float Along – Fill Your Lungs contained a Motor Spirit jam with lyrics, a Han-Tyumi, The Confused Cyborg tease, Theia teases and quotes, and a Muddy Water and Superposition tease. Welcome to an Altered Future was on the printed setlist before Digital Black but was not played.

Before Ice V, which was dedicated to "the incredible country called Greece", Joey told the story of his appearance in the Ice V music video, which was filmed on the island of Idra. The song itself contained Ain't It Funky Now (James Brown) teases. Digital Black was preceded by a tease of A New World. Float Along – Fill Your Lungs contained a Motor Spirit jam with lyrics, a Han-Tyumi, The Confused Cyborg tease, Theia teases and quotes, and a Muddy Water and Superposition tease. Welcome to an Altered Future was on the printed setlist before Digital Black but was not played.

This show featured the debut of Superposition. The Fourth Colour contained a Dripping Tap jam with lyrics. Band manager Michelle's son Laurence joined the band on stage for Straws in the Wind, which contained Phantom Island quotes and Ambrose chanting "free free Palestine". The audience continued the Palestine chants after the song, with the band repeating the slogan once along with them and then again during Minimum Brain Size. Altered Beast III, Alter Me III and Altered Beast IV were played for the first time since 2023-03-23 (115 shows).  Before Stressin', Stu teased Bullet in the Head (Rage Against The Machine). The Book was introduced a song about ancient stuff and featured a short drum solo. Hot Water was played in an alternate synth version for the first time. After Mirage City, Joey shouted out opener King Stingray and thanked guitarist Roy Kellaway for the Yothu Yindi shirt he was wearing. Robot Stop contained Hot Water and Crumbling Castle teases and Alter Me teases and quotes. Sea of Trees contained Hot Water and The Dripping Tap teases, Ice V teases, an Alter Me outro, and the second verse was changed to "I'm feeling kinda shit... but I've got my mates with me and I guess that is that, that’s that". Work This Time contained Mars For The Rich teases.

This show featured the debut of Smoke & Mirrors. The Dripping Tap contained a Chang'e and Head On/Pill tease and an Antarctica vocal tease. Before Flight b741, the band talked about the clouds at the venue, and Joey quoted Field of Vision, and Stu referenced The Land Before Time. Intrasport was played without the synth table. Sense was played in an alternate synth version for the first time. During Grim Reaper, which contained "Black Market Bargain" lyrics and Get Low (Lil Jon) and H.O.O.D. (Kneecap) quotes. Amby called out, "are you not entertained?" in reference to Gladiator. Only Ambrose's verses of Smoke & Mirrors were performed, and the song contained The Grim Reaper teases. Gaia and Gila Monster ended in a drum solos. Before Inner Cell, Stu teased Loyalty.

The Wheel was last played on 2024-05-29 (61 shows). Most Of What I Like was last played on 2024-05-31 (59 show gap). God Is Calling Me Back Home contained Green River (Creedence Clearwater Revival) and Le Risque teases. Le Risque was performed without the typical break between Cavs' and Ambrose's verses. Hog Calling Contest featured sound tech Joe Santarpia, whose birthday it was, doing pig noises. Most Of What I Like contained a Praise You (Fatboy Slim) jam. The Wheel contained Magma teases in the intro. Plastic Boogie contained a Happy Birthday To You tease. Following the song, the band thanked the crowd, everyone who followed them on tour, opening band King Stingray (who were invited onto stage), the crew, and Jason Galea. Hot Wax was introduced as a song by Jason Galea. Venusian 2 contained Hot Wax teases. Following Motor Spirit the band shouted out Allen and Jackson from the stream crew. Rattlesnake contained teases of Billabong Valley and Cellophane. At the end of the show, Ambrose quoted Nookie (Limp Bizkit).

footnote: Debut, with The Chamber Orchestra of Philadelphia
footnotes: ["Debut, with The Chamber Orchestra of Philadelphia"]
shownotes: This show featured the debut of Phantom Island, Deadstick, Lonely Cosmos, Eternal Return, Panpsych, Spacesick, Aerodynamic, Sea of Doubt, and Silent Spirit and was played with The Chamber Orchestra of Philadelphia conducted by Sarah Hicks. During Grow Wings and Fly, which contained Am I In Heaven? teases, Stu thanked the venue, The Chamber Orchestra of Philadelphia, and Sarah Hicks. The Dripping Tap contained Am I In Heaven? teases and was performed without The Chamber Orchestra of Philadelphia. Only parts I, II, and IV of The River were played. Crumbling Castle was played with the outro (without vocals) for the first time. The Dripping Tap was not on the printed setlist, but it and the jam following Grow Wings and Fly functioned as a setbreak for the orchestra.
soundcheck: The River, Crumbling Castle, Dragon, Iron Lung, This Thing, Mars For The Rich

Crumbling Castle was played with the outro (without vocals) for the first time. The Dripping Tap was not on the printed setlist, but it and the jam following Grow Wings and Fly functioned as a setbreak for the orchestra.

footnote: with The Orchestra of St. Luke’s
shownotes: This show was played with The Orchestra of St. Luke’s conducted by Sarah Hicks. During Grow Wings and Fly, Stu thanked The Orchestra of St. Luke’s and Sarah Hicks. Hot Water contained an Am I In Heaven? tease. Only parts I, II, and IV of The River were performed. Mars For The Rich was dedicated to the band's guitar tech Gaspard, who they then sung happy birthday to, followed by Stu asking the crowd if it was anyone else's birthday. Dragon was introduced as a song about "setting shit on fire". Following Dragon, Stu thanked The Orchestra of St. Luke’s and Sarah Hicks again and Ambrose thanked their tour manager Michelle.

jamchart_notes: Hot Water is preceded by an ethereal jam section while The Orchestra of St. Luke's takes a set break which seamlessly leads into a driving intro to the song. The song itself also features extended jamming throughout, including a driving and heavier than usual section.

footnote: with The Orchestra of St. Luke’s
shownotes: This show was played with The Orchestra of St. Luke’s conducted by Sarah Hicks. After Spacesick, Stu thanked The Orchestra of St. Luke’s and Sarah Hicks. Aerodynamic had a lyric changed to reference New York. After Sea of Doubt, Joey mentioned a wall of alumni backstage featuring acts who have previously performed at the venue, including the Beatles, Bob Dylan, Jimi Hendrix, and the Monkees, and the band expressed their desire to be added. During Grow Wings and Fly, Stu thanked The Orchestra of St. Luke’s and Sarah Hicks again. Magma, which didn't include the "rumble" section, contained Am I In Heaven?, The Dripping Tap, and Crumbling Castle teases and ended with a The River outro which contained Sad Pilot, Crumbling Castle, and Evil Death Roll teases. Only parts I, II, and IV of The River were performed. This Thing was introduced by Joey as a song about a personal secret, and included an Incredible (M-Beat) quote. Mars for the Rich was introduced as a song about Elon Musk, and included a lyric change to "you ain't coming here, the Tesla's too dear". After Mars for the Rich, the band noted that the two shows at this venue are the only two on this tour being livestreamed, wished someone a happy birthday, and exclaimed, "free Palestine motherfuckers!" After Iron Lung, the band thanked The Orchestra of St. Luke’s and Sarah Hicks one last time, as well as thanking the audience.

Oddlife was introduced as Cookie's favorite song. Sense was performed in it's synth arrangement and contained The Grim Reaper teases. The Grim Reaper contained Intergalactic, Sabotage, and (You Gotta) Fight for Your Right (To Party!) (all by the Beastie Boys) quotes, and at the end of the song Ambrose thanked the Beastie Boys. Wah Wah contained a The River intro with Crumbling Castle teases and the song itself contained The River quotes, Crumbling Castle teases and Extinction teases and quotes. Let Me Mend The Past, which contained an It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan) quote, was dedicated to Amby's hangover anxieties. Hypertension contained Extinction teases and quotes. Alter Me (unnumbered) was on the setlist before Altered Beast IV but was not played.

jamchart_notes: -> from Set, this Extinction weaves its way through multiple sections of frenetic, glitchy beats. An energetic first two verses leads to a meditative section where Cavs backs off. Gorgeous synth work from Joey and Cookie picks the energy back up before a driving jam emerges and takes the performance to a close.

Wah Wah contained a The River intro with Crumbling Castle teases and the song itself contained The River quotes, Crumbling Castle teases and Extinction teases and quotes. Let Me Mend The Past, which contained an It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan) quote, was dedicated to Amby's hangover anxieties. Hypertension contained Extinction teases and quotes. Alter Me (unnumbered) was on the setlist before Altered Beast IV but was not played.

This show was played with The National Symphony Orchestra conducted by Sarah Hicks. During Grow Wings and Fly, Joey spoke about how even though Phantom Island talks about the band missing their families, the fans make it worth it. Following that, he said Free Palestine. Cut Throat Boogie featured a guitar/harmonica duel between Joey and Ambrose and was unfinished. Boogieman Sam contained Sad Pilot and Cut Throat Boogie teases. Only parts I, II, and IV of The River were performed. Before Dragon, the band mentioned Animal Collective's album Merriweather Post Pavilion, which was named after the venue.

This show was played with The Chicago Philharmonic conducted by Sarah Hicks. Panpsych's lyrics were changed to "Panpsych, fuck yeah". Silent Spirit contained a false start. Grow Wings and Fly contained additional lyrics about the band pissing themselves and a Theia jam with an Am I In Heaven? tease. Theia was performed in it's rock arrangement and contained Vomit Coffin and The Land Before Timeland teases, a Field of Vision jam, Sharp Dressed Man (ZZ Top) teases and quotes, and band introductions. Only parts I, II, and IV of The River were played. Following Crumbling Castle, Joey asked how the lawn was doing and said he couldn't see them (due to the venue layout making it hard to see the stage from the lawn). Stu then said he’d be in the lawn if he was watching. Mars For The Rich had lyrics changed to "Fuck Elon Musk" and "Tesla's too dear". Before Iron Lung, Ambrose thanked Chicago for hot dogs and the Blues Brothers, and Stu and Joey said Free Palestine.

jamchart_notes: After the Chicago Philharmonic left the stage following Grow Wings and Fly (which itself contained a Theia jam), the band meandered into a rock-arrangement Theia. Eventually it launches into some up tempo teases and jamming before settling back into a tasty groove before starting up a Field of Vision jam with some ZZ Top teasing.

Stu then said he’d be in the lawn if he was watching. Mars For The Rich had lyrics changed to "Fuck Elon Musk" and "Tesla's too dear". Before Iron Lung, Ambrose thanked Chicago for hot dogs and the Blues Brothers, and Stu and Joey said Free Palestine.

This show was played with The Chicago Philharmonic conducted by Sarah Hicks. Panpsych's lyrics were changed to "Panpsych, fuck yeah". Silent Spirit contained a false start. Grow Wings and Fly contained additional lyrics about the band pissing themselves and a Theia jam with an Am I In Heaven? tease. Theia was performed in it's rock arrangement and contained Vomit Coffin and The Land Before Timeland teases, a Field of Vision jam, Sharp Dressed Man (ZZ Top) teases and quotes, and band introductions. Only parts I, II, and IV of The River were played. Following Crumbling Castle, Joey asked how the lawn was doing and said he couldn't see them (due to the venue layout making it hard to see the stage from the lawn). Stu then said he’d be in the lawn if he was watching. Mars For The Rich had lyrics changed to "Fuck Elon Musk" and "Tesla's too dear". Before Iron Lung, Ambrose thanked Chicago for hot dogs and the Blues Brothers, and Stu and Joey said Free Palestine.

This show was performed with The Hollywood Bowl Orchestra, conducted by Sarah Hicks. Grow Wings and Fly contained Plastic Boogie teases and a Hypertension jam with The Dripping Tap and Magma teases. At the start of the jam, Joey asked the pit what they were eating for dinner and said he hoped it wasn’t fish. Stu then joked that everyone had food poisoning before saying that it actually looked good. The band followed up by asking if the crowd had any desserts to share. A fan gave Cook tiramisu after he asked for it. He ate a bite and gave it back, and Stu joked that he put death cap mushrooms in it. Ambrose took a bucket of popcorn and ate it, later cleaning it off the stage after it went everywhere. This Thing was introduced by Joey as a song about Los Angeles, followed by him quoting I Love L.A. (Randy Newman). Mars for the Rich was dedicated to Elon Musk, with Stu saying the band decided all billionaires are going to prison (except Cook because he was chill) and had lyrics changed to “Tesla’s too dear” alongside an added “Free Palestine”. Ambrose gave a shout-out to his mom before Iron Lung. During the song, he walked into the garden pit and dumped a bucket of popcorn on himself.

footnote: with the San Diego Symphony Orchestra
shownotes: This show was played with The San Diego Symphony Orchestra conducted by Sarah Hicks. Sea of Doubt was introduced as "another one about the ocean." Grow Wings and Fly contained Motor Spirit, Wah Wah and Witchcraft teases. Before The River Stu received a tie-dye suit jacket from the audience. Mars For the Rich had lyric changed to "the Tesla's too dear" and was dedicated to David Bowie. Iron Lung contained Am I In Heaven? teases.
soundcheck: Spacesick, The River
venuename: The Rady Shell at Jacobs Park

shownotes: This show featured the debut of Police Truck (Dead Kennedys). Before the band's set, Roberto Mukaro Borrero of the Guainía Taíno Tribe gave a land acknowledgment to the Ute people and performed a Native American song. Gamma Knife contained a Motor Spirit tease. Mr. Beat contained Money For Nothing (Dire Straits) and People-Vultures teases. A fan named Dog was brought on to do the Nuclear Fusion intro. Oddlife was paused after beginning for approximately 10 minutes due to a medical emergency in the audience. Following Intrasport, which was played without the synth table, Joey said he felt like Liam Gallagher of Oasis (as he doesn't play his guitar, and has a tambourine during live performances). Following Extinction, the band shouted out White Fence and talked about their 2014 tour with the band. I'm Not In Your Mind contained an Extinction tease. Cellophane contained a What If (The Murlocs) tease from Ambrose. Inner Cell was introduced as a song about dictators and how they're losers. Loyalty contained a Horology tease. Venusian 2 was introduced as a song about "going to Venus and being set on fire." After Venusian 2, the band thanked Cavs for playing at a high altitude for 3 hours, with Stu saying to get him some oxygen and jellybeans. Following Witchcraft the band shouted out Gaspard, the band's sound tech, who brought them tequila shots. Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs and contained Am I In Heaven? and Magma teases. Police Truck featured Dead Kennedys vocalist Jello Biafra on vocals and contained a Robot Stop tease. Before the song, Joey said the Dead Kennedys were a huge inspiration on their early material (with Stu giving Trench Foot as an example), and Ambrose told a story about how they applied to open for the band (who Jello called the 'phony Dead Kennedys') in Melbourne, but were declined. Following the song, Stu said "Free Palestine and Fuck the Police!" Evil Death Roll was not on the printed setlist and contained Field of Vision quotes, an Iron Lung jam with lyrics, and Am I In Heaven? teases. During the slower quiet section (and subsequent Iron Lung jam) Ambrose directed the crowd to sit down and jump up when the song kicked back in. At the end of the show, Joey said Free Palestine. During The Songs For Kids Band!'s set earlier in the day, Stu, Lucas, and Cavs joined the band for a cover of Flight b741. KGLW.net Staff Notes: We send our deepest sympathy and condolences to the friends and family of Matt Gawiak, who passed away after collapsing during this show. The community that has developed around this band is a type of family and a loss to any of us is a loss to all of us. Our hearts go out to all who knew and loved him.

shownotes: This show featured the debut of Police Truck (Dead Kennedys). Before the band's set, Roberto Mukaro Borrero of the Guainía Taíno Tribe gave a land acknowledgment to the Ute people and performed a Native American song. Gamma Knife contained a Motor Spirit tease. Mr. Beat contained Money For Nothing (Dire Straits) and People-Vultures teases. A fan named Dog was brought on to do the Nuclear Fusion intro. Oddlife was paused after beginning for approximately 10 minutes due to a medical emergency in the audience. Following Intrasport, which was played without the synth table, Joey said he felt like Liam Gallagher of Oasis (as he doesn't play his guitar, and has a tambourine during live performances). Following Extinction, the band shouted out White Fence and talked about their 2014 tour with the band. I'm Not In Your Mind contained an Extinction tease. Cellophane contained a What If (The Murlocs) tease from Ambrose. Inner Cell was introduced as a song about dictators and how they're losers. Loyalty contained a Horology tease. Venusian 2 was introduced as a song about "going to Venus and being set on fire." After Venusian 2, the band thanked Cavs for playing at a high altitude for 3 hours, with Stu saying to get him some oxygen and jellybeans. Following Witchcraft the band shouted out Gaspard, the band's sound tech, who brought them tequila shots. Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs and contained Am I In Heaven? and Magma teases. Police Truck featured Dead Kennedys vocalist Jello Biafra on vocals and contained a Robot Stop tease. Before the song, Joey said the Dead Kennedys were a huge inspiration on their early material (with Stu giving Trench Foot as an example), and Ambrose told a story about how they applied to open for the band (who Jello called the 'phony Dead Kennedys') in Melbourne, but were declined. Following the song, Stu said "Free Palestine and Fuck the Police!" Evil Death Roll was not on the printed setlist and contained Field of Vision quotes, an Iron Lung jam with lyrics, and Am I In Heaven? teases. During the slower quiet section (and subsequent Iron Lung jam) Ambrose directed the crowd to sit down and jump up when the song kicked back in. At the end of the show, Joey said Free Palestine. During The Songs For Kids Band!'s set earlier in the day, Stu, Lucas, and Cavs joined the band for a cover of Flight b741. KGLW.net Staff Notes: We send our deepest sympathy and condolences to the friends and family of Matt Gawiak, who passed away after collapsing during this show. The community that has developed around this band is a type of family and a loss to any of us is a loss to all of us. Our hearts go out to all who knew and loved him.

jamchart_notes: A pretty unique performance featuring Jello Biafra of the Dead Kennedys on vocals. Before the song the band talks about being inspired by their work.

Slow Jam 1 featured Ambrose on saxophone and contained an Am I In Heaven? tease. Empty was performed for the first time since 2014-12-19 (644 show gap), and was performed with flute for the first time. Hot Wax was played by request of Wayne Gordon (who recorded I'm In Your Mind Fuzz and Quarters! at Daptone Studios and made a brief appearance on stage) and contained an Am I In Heaven? tease. A fan named Callum was brought onto stage to play Superbug on Stu's guitar instead of Stu. Magenta Mountain contained Forty Six & 2 (Tool) and Magma teases. The Grim Reaper contained Forty Six & 2 (Tool) teases and Intergalactic, Sabotage (both Beastie Boys), and Torture Chamber quotes. Following The Grim Reaper, Joey said he was ripping off Wes Borland (of Limp Bizkit), and Ambrose said he needed a red hat (Fred Durst's signature hat). Swan Song contained Magenta Mountain teases and quotes. Le Risque featured Jay Weinberg on drums instead of Cavs. Ice V featured Joey on flute during the intro and contained a quote of Aqualung (Jethro Tull) and teases of Ants & Bats and Blindness (The Fall). Raw Feel contained Am I In Heaven? teases. Hot Water contained I'm In Your Mind and Robot Stop teases.  Following Hot Water, Joey shouted out festival act The Mystery Lights, saying they were the first band they co-headlined with in America. Boogieman Sam contained The Bitter Boogie, Whole Lotta Rosie (AC/DC) and Riff Raff (AC/DC) teases. During Straws In The Wind, Ambrose walked through the crowd to Jason at the soundboard and when he returned to stage, he did an impression of Fred Durst. After Straws In The Wind, the band pretended it was Jason's birthday and sang Happy Birthday to him. During Pleura, Joey said "Fuck Donald Trump", with Ambrose responding "Juicy [Jason] for President!" Dimathaya Burarrwanga and Yimila Gurruwiwi from King Stingray joined the band for Rattlesnake on yiḏaki (didgeridoo), and Dimathaya quoted Treaty (Yothu Yindi). At the end of the show, Joey said Free Palestine and Ambrose led the crowd in a chant of "Free Free Palestine".

Boogieman Sam contained The Bitter Boogie, Whole Lotta Rosie (AC/DC) and Riff Raff (AC/DC) teases. During Straws In The Wind, Ambrose walked through the crowd to Jason at the soundboard and when he returned to stage, he did an impression of Fred Durst. After Straws In The Wind, the band pretended it was Jason's birthday and sang Happy Birthday to him. During Pleura, Joey said "Fuck Donald Trump", with Ambrose responding "Juicy [Jason] for President!" Dimathaya Burarrwanga and Yimila Gurruwiwi from King Stingray joined the band for Rattlesnake on yiḏaki (didgeridoo), and Dimathaya quoted Treaty (Yothu Yindi). At the end of the show, Joey said Free Palestine and Ambrose led the crowd in a chant of "Free Free Palestine".

Slow Jam 1 featured Ambrose on saxophone and contained an Am I In Heaven? tease. Empty was performed for the first time since 2014-12-19 (644 show gap), and was performed with flute for the first time. Hot Wax was played by request of Wayne Gordon (who recorded I'm In Your Mind Fuzz and Quarters! at Daptone Studios and made a brief appearance on stage) and contained an Am I In Heaven? tease. Boogieman Sam contained The Bitter Boogie, Whole Lotta Rosie (AC/DC) and Riff Raff (AC/DC) teases. During Straws In The Wind, Ambrose walked through the crowd to Jason at the soundboard and when he returned to stage, he did an impression of Fred Durst. After Straws In The Wind, the band pretended it was Jason's birthday and sang Happy Birthday to him. During Pleura, Joey said "Fuck Donald Trump", with Ambrose responding "Juicy [Jason] for President!" Dimathaya Burarrwanga and Yimila Gurruwiwi from King Stingray joined the band for Rattlesnake on yiḏaki (didgeridoo), and Dimathaya quoted Treaty (Yothu Yindi). At the end of the show, Joey said Free Palestine and Ambrose led the crowd in a chant of "Free Free Palestine".

Slow Jam 1 featured Ambrose on saxophone and contained an Am I In Heaven? tease. Empty was performed for the first time since 2014-12-19 (644 show gap), and was performed with flute for the first time. Hot Wax was played by request of Wayne Gordon (who recorded I'm In Your Mind Fuzz and Quarters! at Daptone Studios and made a brief appearance on stage) and contained an Am I In Heaven? tease. A fan named Callum was brought onto stage to play Superbug on Stu's guitar instead of Stu. Magenta Mountain contained Forty Six & 2 (Tool) and Magma teases. The Grim Reaper contained Forty Six & 2 (Tool) teases and Intergalactic, Sabotage (both Beastie Boys), and Torture Chamber quotes. Following The Grim Reaper, Joey said he was ripping off Wes Borland (of Limp Bizkit), and Ambrose said he needed a red hat (Fred Durst's signature hat). Swan Song contained Magenta Mountain teases and quotes. Le Risque featured Jay Weinberg on drums instead of Cavs. Ice V featured Joey on flute during the intro and contained a quote of Aqualung (Jethro Tull) and teases of Ants & Bats and Blindness (The Fall). Raw Feel contained Am I In Heaven? teases. Hot Water contained I'm In Your Mind and Robot Stop teases.  Following Hot Water, Joey shouted out festival act The Mystery Lights, saying they were the first band they co-headlined with in America. Boogieman Sam contained The Bitter Boogie, Whole Lotta Rosie (AC/DC) and Riff Raff (AC/DC) teases. During Straws In The Wind, Ambrose walked through the crowd to Jason at the soundboard and when he returned to stage, he did an impression of Fred Durst. After Straws In The Wind, the band pretended it was Jason's birthday and sang Happy Birthday to him. During Pleura, Joey said "Fuck Donald Trump", with Ambrose responding "Juicy [Jason] for President!" Dimathaya Burarrwanga and Yimila Gurruwiwi from King Stingray joined the band for Rattlesnake on yiḏaki (didgeridoo), and Dimathaya quoted Treaty (Yothu Yindi). At the end of the show, Joey said Free Palestine and Ambrose led the crowd in a chant of "Free Free Palestine".

showtitle: Yadaki Psych Set at Field of Vision
shownotes: This show was performed on the Timeland Stage after King Gizzard's headlining set. The Ice V and Lookin' Out jams featured Joey, Ambrose, and Lucas. The first jam contained Treaty (Yothu Yindi) quotes and unknown lyrics. The second jam also contained unknown lyrics. The Ice V jam contained multiple sections of lyrical improv and freestyling from Dimathaya, unknown possibly improvised lyrics from Ambrose, including the "black market bargain spinal cords" lines found in the previous Europe tour, Gloria (T.H.E.M.), Shake Your Daughter (Revolver & Sun), Stoned Mullet, Killing in the Name (Rage Against The Machine), Torture Chamber, and Whole Lotta Love (Led Zeppelin) quotes. The Lookin' Out jam contained Hypertension teases and Treaty and Ghost Spirits (both Yothu Yindi) quotes.

The band dressed in drag for the third annual drag night. Theia, Sad Pilot, Most Of What I Like, and Aerodynamic were performed acoustic. Theia contained The River and The Land Before Timeland teases. Most Of What I Like contained Praise You (Fatboy Slim) teases and was dedicated by Joey to his wife Edith. Aerodynamic was played for the first time without an orchestra. Before Venusian 1, the band said if they should have soundchecked it and joked that if they messed it up, they would play Stoned Mullet instead. Following Venusian 1, Joey quoted Chop Suey (System of a Down) and Lucas shouted out festival act Yvie Oddly. Her and I (Slow Jam 2) contained You Can Be Your Silhouette, Am I In Heaven?, The River, Blindness (The Fall), Crumbling Castle, and Monkey Disco (Babe Rainbow) teases. Following Her and I, the band announced that the festival would be played again next year, and shouted out their manager Michelle Cable and the fans at the festival. O.N.E. was introduced as Duran Duran's favorite song. Before Billabong Valley, Joey mentioned Australian rapper RAED. During The Silver Cord, Stu vamped on the lyrics "let's get fucked up" and "everybody wants to save us." Following Daily Blues, Ambrose quoted Scarface (Push It to the Limit) (Paul Engemann). Float Along - Fill Your Lungs was dedicated to Matt Gawiak, a fan that passed away during the show night 1 of the festival and contained a Dragon jam. Ambrose led the crowd in a chant of 'Matt' following Float Along - Fill Your Lungs. The Dripping Tap was also dedicated to Matt Gawaik. Rats In The sky was on the printed setlist after The Garden Goblin but was skipped.

During The Silver Cord, Stu vamped on the lyrics "let's get fucked up" and "everybody wants to save us." Following Daily Blues, Ambrose quoted Scarface (Push It to the Limit) (Paul Engemann). Float Along - Fill Your Lungs was dedicated to Matt Gawiak, a fan that passed away during the show night 1 of the festival and contained a Dragon jam. Ambrose led the crowd in a chant of 'Matt' following Float Along - Fill Your Lungs. The Dripping Tap was also dedicated to Matt Gawaik. Rats In The sky was on the printed setlist after The Garden Goblin but was skipped.

The band dressed in drag for the third annual drag night. Theia, Sad Pilot, Most Of What I Like, and Aerodynamic were performed acoustic. Theia contained The River and The Land Before Timeland teases. Most Of What I Like contained Praise You (Fatboy Slim) teases and was dedicated by Joey to his wife Edith. Aerodynamic was played for the first time without an orchestra. Before Venusian 1, the band said if they should have soundchecked it and joked that if they messed it up, they would play Stoned Mullet instead. Following Venusian 1, Joey quoted Chop Suey (System of a Down) and Lucas shouted out festival act Yvie Oddly. Her and I (Slow Jam 2) contained You Can Be Your Silhouette, Am I In Heaven?, The River, Blindness (The Fall), Crumbling Castle, and Monkey Disco (Babe Rainbow) teases. This show was a free performance announced 5 days beforehand and featured the debuts of JOJAM, 2.02 Killer Year, Blue Morpho and Butterfly 3000. The Silver Cord was unfinshed.  Set featured Ambrose on sax. Joey said Free Palestine during 2.02 Killer Year. Extinction featured Joey on guitar. The Grim Reaper contained nearly all the lyrics of Torture Chamber, "Black Market Bargain" lyrics, Intergalactic and Sabotage (Beastie Boys) quotes, and Rollin' (Limp Bizkit) quotes. Flamethrower (Outro) was on the printed setlist between Butterfly 3000 and The Grim Reaper but was not played.
soundcheck: Flamethrower (Outro), The Silver Cord, Kepler-22b, The Grim Reaper

shownotes: This show featured the debut of Hell's Itch, which was also the synth version and contained Joey's and Stu's final verses only, and "LUSEQ". Theia contained a Hot Water tease from Stu on electric flute. Hot Water contained The Dripping Tap teases. Slow Jam 1 contained a Hell's Itch jam. Hell's Itch contained a substantial lyrical portion of Pure Shores (All Saints) before reprising the "finally red" chorus. Murder of the Universe was performed in an electronic version for the first time.

Bullant set after King Gizzard's show that same night. Gaspard and Jackson came on stage for Crush (Worldcolour). Ambrose and Lucas came on stage after The Queen (M.I.T.A.) and were later joined by Cavs and the crew during Iruka (Alarico). At the end of the show Joey did a strongman pose and was kissed by Cavs and Lucas.

Gilgamesh was labeled "Frankenstein" on the printed setlist. Only a small portion of Superposition without lyrics was played. Cyboogie was last played 2020-02-21 (240 show gap). During the jam out of The Garden Goblin, Amby asked the crowd if they wanted a drum solo, to which the crowd responded yes, and heavily drum-based improv followed. Only Ambrose's verses in Smoke & Mirrors, which contained all of The Grim Reaper rapped over it as well as Bonkers (Dizzee Rascal) quotes, were performed. Magma was played electronic for the first time and contained a Smoke & Mirrors intro, an After Midnight (J.J. Cale) quote, and Smoke & Mirrors teases and quotes. At the end of the show, Amby chanted Bullant and shouted out Jason Galea.

Only Ambrose's verses in Smoke & Mirrors, which contained all of The Grim Reaper rapped over it as well as Bonkers (Dizzee Rascal) quotes, were performed. Magma was played electronic for the first time and contained a Smoke & Mirrors intro, an After Midnight (J.J. Cale) quote, and Smoke & Mirrors teases and quotes. At the end of the show, Amby chanted Bullant and shouted out Jason Galea.

jamchart_notes: A massive version of Garden Goblin that contains ambient improv interludes during both the start and end.

This show featured the debut of Dreams. 2.02 Killer Year contained Shanghai teases. Shanghai contained Grow Wings and Fly teases and quotes, as well as the "let's get real high" refrain. Kepler-22b had it's lyrics changed to "dancin' is good for ya". At the end of the show, Joey said Free Palestine. The Silver Cord was on the printed setlist as the final song but was skipped for time.

jamchart_notes: The song starts with a haunting ambient intro featuring Stu on electric flute. Eventually the familiar Shanghai melody comes fully in, and by the end of the song elements of Grow Wings and Fly are present, leading into a groovy section to cap off the longest rendition of Shanghai to date.

This show was played with Covent Garden Sinfonia conducted by Chad Kelly. Before Phantom Island, the band said it was the best day of their lives and shouted out the orchestra and conductor Chad Kelly. After Eternal Return, Joey asked if anyone in the crowd was at the previous Brixton shows. Aerodynamic featured a lyric change to "watch the whole world go by". The Grow Wings and Fly jam had Hot Water, Head On/Pill and The Great Chain of Being teases. This Thing featured an Incredible (M Beat) quote. Mars For the Rich was introduced by Joey as a song about "how much of a massive fucking cunt" Elon Musk is and had a lyric change to "fuck Elon Musk!" Joey said "Free Palestine" after Dragon and Iron Lung.
soundcheck: Lonely Cosmos, The River, Crumbling Castle

This show featured the live debut of Fishing For Fishies. Ice V had Muddy Water, Blindness (The Fall) and Extinction teases, as well as Joey playing riffs from various Set jams on guitar. A cake decorated to look like the Flight b741 album cover was brought on stage during Dreams. The Grim Reaper had a lyric change to "the grim reaper shits on your chest" and had quotes from Torture Chamber, Sabotage (Beastie Boys), Bonkers (Dizzee Rascal) and Rollin' (Limp Bizkit). During the song Ambrose pushed his face into the cake. Cavs went crowd surfing during Fishing For Fishies. Ambrose introduced Smoke & Mirrors as a song about someone who "did us dirty" and thanked the audience for the cake. Ambrose went crowd surfing during the song. Magma, which didn't include the "rumble" section, contained a Smoke & Mirrors jam with lyrics.

This show featured the live debut of Fishing For Fishies. Ice V had Muddy Water, Blindness (The Fall) and Extinction teases, as well as Joey playing riffs from various Set jams on guitar. This show was played with Sinfonia Rotterdam conducted by Chad Kelly. Panpsych's lyrics were changed to "Panpsych, fuck yeah." Aerodynamic's lyrics were changed to "sit here and watch Max Verstappen float by." Following Silent Spirit, Joey dedicted Grow Wings and Fly to his daughter. Following Grow Wings and Fly, Joey shouted out conductor Chad Kelly and Amby shouted out Sinfonia Amsterdam (which Stu then corrected to Rotterdam).  the intermission jam contained an extensive The Land Before Timeland jam. Evil Death Roll contained a Robot Stop tease. The River contained Thunderstruck (AC/DC) teases. Following Crumbling Castle, which contained a The River tease, Joey shouted out Cavs. Mars For The Rich had lyrics changed to "Fuck Donald Trump" and "Tesla's too dear".

Theia contained a Gilgamesh quote and The Silver Cord lyrics from Theia's extended mix. Shanghai contained a Grow Wings and Fly jam with lyrics. During Swan Song, the band's electronics cut out repeatedly leading Joey to call for a drum solo, which Cavs duly provided while the band troubleshooted. Extinction contained Blue Morpho quotes toward the end. During Blue Morpho, the band's electronics crashed again and the song was abandoned. During the pause the crowd called for a drum solo, which Cavs returned to the stage to provide once more and which contained Dragon teases. The subsequent "...Maddy Jam..." included opener Maddie Ashman on cello; early within the jam, Stu said, "if you like this, it means you're avant-garde". Set contained Gila Monster and Extinction quotes. Ambrose performed the verses of Smoke & Mirrors out of order, included all the lyrics from The Grim Reaper as well as Torture Chamber quotes, and changed the lyrics to "another little man trying to live the dream" as well as "Grim Reaper shits on your chest".

Due to the technical issues plaguing the first half of the show, the setlist was changed considerably from what was on the printed setlists, which read: Swan Song, Candles, Extinction, Blue Morpho, Maddy Jam, No Body, Slow Jam I, Magma, Smoke and Mirrors, Set.

This show featured the debut of No Body.  Cyboogie contained Perihelion teases and quotes. Gilgamesh was labeled "Frankenstein" on the printed setlist.  Kepler-22b was labeled "Couple 22-Beers" on the printed setlist and had a lyric change to "dancin' is good for ya". The Grim Reaper featured Ambrose performing the verses in the mosh pit and contained Killing in the Name (Rage Against the Machine), Sabotage (Beastie Boys), Bonkers (Dizzee Rascal), You Can Do It (Ice Cube), Get Low (Lil Jon and the East Side Boyz), Torture Chamber and Black Market Bargain quotes, as well as Smoke & Mirrors quotes and teases. The Silver Cord contained partial lyrics from the extended mix.

jamchart_notes: This is the longest version of Kepler to date. Has an extended intro that sees Amby on Sax. The song has some really funky electronic jamming, some frenetic beats and high energy drums, and a section with robotic sounding melodies.

This show was played with The Sydney Symphony Orchestra conducted by Chad Kelly.  Intermission Jam contained Hypertension teases.  The River contained teases of Thunderstruck (AC/DC) and Her and I.  Dragon was introduced as a song about capitalism.  Before Iron Lung Joey said Free Palestine.

This show was played with The Sydney Symphony Orchestra conducted by Chad Kelly. Before the band went on stage, they were introduced by Uncle Michael West during a freewheeling acknowledgement of country, fauna, and waterways. After Spacesick, Stu shouted out the orchestra and said how lucky they felt to play there, and Joey pointed out the audience could have gone to see Franz Ferdinand instead, who were performing that evening on the steps immediately outside the Sydney Opera House, but chose them; Aerodynamic then contained a lyric change to "just gonna sit here and watch Franz Ferdinand roll by". After Sea of Doubt, the band thanked Chad. Stu shouted out both the orchestra and Chad again at the end of Grow Wings and Fly. As the intermission jam started, Joey instructed the band to play more atonally as he related the story of playing the same venue when opening for The Drones on 2013-04-27, being unable to find a bathroom and choosing to urinate on that very stage. Daily Blues contained Green River teases. Jackson, one-half of the usual stream team and its only present member at this show, was shouted out after Daily Blues. Happy Birthday was performed by the orchestra for Lucas; afterwards Stu described him as one of his oldest friends and said he brought the band together. Crumbling Castle contained a lyric change to "thank you Gaspard", in reference to the band's guitar tech. Stu shouted out the orchestra and Chad after Crumbling Castle. Joey told another urination story before This Thing, which was then introduced as a song about that story. This Thing contained a lyric change to "and that is what makes this hard quiz" in reference to the Australian game show Hard Quiz, where the band was recently selected as a speciality topic by a fan Finn; who the band also joked about spotting in a small mosh pit that had formed at the end of an aisle on the venue floor. Before Mars for the Rich, Stu said "fuck Jeff Bezos" and repeatedly called for all billionaires to be sent to jail. The song itself contained a lyric change to "the Tesla's too dear". At the end, Ambrose said "thank you Sexy Alien". Dragon was dedicated to people who know about drop B and described as a song by Limp Bizkit; before the song started, Blind (Korn) was teased. At the end of the show, Chad and the orchestra were thanked again, along with Sam, Terrence and Jason.

At the end, Ambrose said "thank you Sexy Alien". Dragon was dedicated to people who know about drop B and described as a song by Limp Bizkit; before the song started, Blind (Korn) was teased. At the end of the show, Chad and the orchestra were thanked again, along with Sam, Terrence and Jason.

As the intermission jam started, Joey instructed the band to play more atonally as he related the story of playing the same venue when opening for The Drones on 2013-04-27, being unable to find a bathroom and choosing to urinate on that very stage. Daily Blues contained Green River teases. Jackson, one-half of the usual stream team and its only present member at this show, was shouted out after Daily Blues. Happy Birthday was performed by the orchestra for Lucas; afterwards Stu described him as one of his oldest friends and said he brought the band together. Crumbling Castle contained a lyric change to "thank you Gaspard", in reference to the band's guitar tech. Stu shouted out the orchestra and Chad after Crumbling Castle. Joey told another urination story before This Thing, which was then introduced as a song about that story. This Thing contained a lyric change to "and that is what makes this hard quiz" in reference to the Australian game show Hard Quiz, where the band was recently selected as a speciality topic by a fan Finn; who the band also joked about spotting in a small mosh pit that had formed at the end of an aisle on the venue floor. Before Mars for the Rich, Stu said "fuck Jeff Bezos" and repeatedly called for all billionaires to be sent to jail. The song itself contained a lyric change to "the Tesla's too dear". At the end, Ambrose said "thank you Sexy Alien". Dragon was dedicated to people who know about drop B and described as a song by Limp Bizkit; before the song started, Blind (Korn) was teased. At the end of the show, Chad and the orchestra were thanked again, along with Sam, Terrence and Jason.

Joey said "Fuck Peter Dutton" during the intro of Pleura. Before Magma Joey shouted "Light 'em up, T!" then said that he's always wanted to say that in Australia. Field of Vision contained a quote of Wipeout (The Surfaris).  Dirt was played for the first time since 2024-11-16 (50 show gap) and was played all electric for the first time. During The Dripping Tap, which contained Magma, Machine Gun (Jimi Hendrix) and Cellophane teases, Stu’s amp broke so Joey told a story while another amp was brought out. After Witchcraft, Cavs performed a drum solo while the rest of the band took a break, followed by Cavs taking his own break. The Mind Fuzz suite was introduced as a song by Jimi Hendrix called I'm In Your Mind Fuzz.  I'm Not In Your Mind contained Trapdoor, Machine Gun (Jimi Hendrix) and The Dripping Tap teases.

Before the band went on stage, they were introduced by Uncle Michael West. During Motor Spirit, Stu shouted out "I love heavy metal!". Following Work This Time Joey thanked Jimi Hendrix.  The tuneup for Le Risque contained a Hot Water tease.  Before Hot Water, Stu announced that "this song is not called Hog Calling Contest" and invited sound tech Joe Santarpia to do pig noises, which he declined. Hot Water contained Whole Lotta Rosie (AC/DC) teases.  Intrasport, which was played without the synth table, was dedicated to Darren Hayes from Savage Garden. Rattlesnake featured Dimathaya Burarrwanga and Ngalakan "Billy" Wanambi from King Stingray on yiḏaki (didgeridoo) and contained Ghost Spirits (Yothu Yindi) quotes by Dimathaya. Hog Calling Contest was on the written setlist after Le Risque but was not played due to issues with Stu's equipment.

Hog Calling Contest was on the written setlist after Le Risque but was not played due to issues with Stu's equipment.

jamchart_notes: Particularly heavy jam, with throat singing from Stu and screaming from Amby.

The show was preceded by a welcome to country by Lafe Charlton. The intro to Nuclear Fusion was done by a fan named Keira. Following K.G.L.W. (Outro), Stu shouted out Jackson and Ducky from the stream team, and then said he wishes he was Ducky, then the band said he was Dougie from Twin Peaks. Am I In Heaven? contained Her and I (Slow Jam 2), Evil Death Roll, Inner Cell, The Dripping Tap, Head On/Pill, and Sad Pilot teases and had lyrics changed to "Am I In Brisbane?". Before Inner Cell, the band invited an audience member to do backflips on stage whenever he wanted, and he came up during the song. Inner Cell had lyrics changed to reference the backflip guy, and the song was stopped midway through because the band couldn't stop laughing at him. Before Predator X, which had a lyric changed to "Redditor X", the band shouted out Jason Galea. Following the song, the band sung the backwards man song from Freddy Got Fingered, but changed to the backflip man, and Joey teased Sultans of Swing (Dire Straits). Antarctica contained Hot Water teases. Before Hog Calling Contest, the band brought back the backflip guy as well as sound engineer Joe Santarpia to do pig noises. Gamma Knife contained Motor Spirit quotes.

jamchart_notes: Playful “Am I in Brisbane?” lyric changes and inspired jamming highlight this lengthy version. The band quietly build the song as they trickle teases in its calm intro before launching into the song proper. An extended section of mellow space unravels out of a dense, frenetic jam that sees some of the band’s freest playing all year before the song’s final verses kick off more high energy jamming with rapid-fire teases.

jamchart_notes: An ambient jam minus Cavs (bathroom break) starts off this Ice V which includes the intro of Panpsych. Cavs returns before they launch into the longest non-electronic rendition of the song yet, with plenty of teases.

Altered Beast I contained an Alter Me intro.  Alter Me II was played for the first time since 2022-10-31 (177 shows) and contained Hypertension quotes and teases and Forty Six & 2 (Tool) teases. Altered Beast IV contained Hypertension teases.  Hypertension contained Riff Raff (AC/DC), Forty Six & 2 (Tool) and Sad Pilot teases. Muddy Water and The Bitter Boogie were performed as a mashup with parts of each song interspersed throughout the other.  Sad Pilot contained teases of The Bitter Boogie and Machine Gun (Jimi Hendrix).  The band's guitar tech Gaspard, introduced as Gary, played drums on Le Risque while Cavs performed his verse from the front of the stage and stayed on drums for the rest of the song.  Motor Spirit was introduced as a song about cars.  Ambrose quoted The Next Episode (Dr. Dre & Snoop Dogg) in Motor Spirit after the band took shots following Gila Monster. At the end of the show the band shouted out opener Barkaa and Joey said "from the river to the sea, free Palestine."  The written setlist had Raw Feel listed as Raw Phil, Muddy Water and The Bitter Boogie listed as Muddy Boogie // Bitter Water, Sad Pilot listed as Rad Pilot and Hot Wax listed as Hot Phacts.
